---
title: "IO/NGO GLOA 101"
author: "Byunghwan Son <br> George Mason University"
date: " "
#########################################
linkcolor: red
citecolor: blue
urlcolor: blueß
output: 
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    seal: false
    nature:
      ratio: '16:9'
      highlightStyle: github
      mathjax: "default"  
  #    self-contained: TRUE
  chakra: css/libs/remark-latest.min.js
css: ["default", 
      "css/footer.css", 
      "css/color.css", 
      # "css/xaringan-themer.css",
      default-fonts]
lib_dir: css/libs
beforeInit: "css/macros.js"
# seal: false  
#bibliography: /Users/bson3/Dropbox/latex/mybib2.bib
#remotes::install_github('yihui/xaringan', upgrade = TRUE)

---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# to use FontAwesome
#htmltools::tagList(rmarkdown::html_dependency_font_awesome())
library(fs)
library(fontawesome)
# library(magick)  
library(ggplot2)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, error=F, echo=F)
options(htmltools.preserve.raw = FALSE)
```

class: center, middle, inverse
background-image: url(https://i.makeagif.com/media/10-02-2021/vJ2tpK.gif)
background-size: 110%

# .Large[.Yellow[**International Organizations & NGOs**]]

# **GLOA 101**

---

class: inverse
background-image: url(https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/blackboardBackground.jpg)
background-size: 110%

# **Overview**

## What we talk about:
1. .Large[Concepts / types of IOs & NGOs]
  - .Large[more emphasis on **NGOs** as we covered IOs quite a bit]
2. .Large[Criticisms]

## You will be able to:
1. .Large[provide .pink[examples] for each kind of IOs/NGOs]
2. .Large[explain the diﬀerences between the .yellow[roles] that diﬀerent kinds of IOs/NGOs play]

---

.full-width-blue[
# What are they? The characteristics.
]
<br>
--

.pull-left[

# IOs `r fa("flag", fill='brown')`

- .Large[governments are primary .red[members]]
- .Large[often a result of political .green[compromise]]
- .Large[.orange[coordinating] government policies]
- .Large[often .purple[take over] nation states' roles]
- .Large[almost always based on treaties/agreements; belongs to a hierarchy]
]

--

.pull-right[

# NGOs `r fa("hand", fill='red')`

- .Large[.red[no] direct government participation; .green[individuals/communities] gathered around a .pink[common] goal]
- .Large[usually aim at .orange[changing] gov't policies]
- .Large[emerged where nation states .purple[retreated]] 
- .Large[‘legally’ constructed: listed/recognized; non-profit; .red[not] simply 'activist groups']

]

---

## Are IOs '.hl3[neutral]' while NGOs '.hl2[biased]'? <br> .tiny[Benson & Kathman (2014): based on the .purple[UNSC resolutions]]

--

.pull-left[
![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/Benson2014_1.png)

]

--

.pull-right[

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/Benson2014_2.png)

]


---

background-image: url(https://media.tenor.com/3FOVil5NxugAAAAM/game-of-thrones-shame.gif)
background-size: 30%
background-position: right

# .hl1[How do NGOs Operate?] .tiny[only `r fa("hand-point-right", fill='red')` NAME and SHAME??]

--

- .LARGE[monitoring and gathering attention & <br> disseminating .purple[information]] 
--

- .LARGE[helping draft international <br>conventions/treaties] <br>.Large[e.g., Mine Ban Treaty]
--

- .LARGE[providing expert advice] <br> .Large[e.g., Amnesty Int'l `r fa("arrow-right")` UN sanctions]
--

- .LARGE[performing functions states don’t] <br>.Large[e.g., MSF; Save the Children]
--

- .LARGE[boycotting]


---

.full-width-blue[
# Types of NGOs
]
<br>
--

### Human rights NGOs: .orange[Amnesty International; Human Rights Watch; MSF] 

--

- .large[advocate for better human rights standard]
- .large[report human rights abuses]

### Development NGOs .blue[ConcernWorldWide; Oxfarm; WaterAid]

--

- .large[provide aid to support development]
- .large[diﬀerent than Human rights NGOs?]

### Environmental NGOs: .green[Green Peace; WWF; Climate Action Network]

--

- .large[advocacy/reports]
- .large[technical advice]

---

class: inverse
background-image: url(https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEgwqJ6i3lfAJT_6ALbnU5_WpeJlbTBagPPYO_e5B-uFyfjSZ7PBdW9k07wewupfGTY-jzl52YrNXCD7BD3RotjPsjn_rJClwJOMTZ47c4lMlg1FLwU9qIYtVDGu3JdR6O3sbo8AZGt1H34JBg0S4M4PjblGL_tQWwZgqBHdraQiMMKvQzGaoYb0dYSLcLsx/s500/tumblr_mtny5nFTMA1rey868o1_500.gif)
background-size: contain
# But uh.....


---

# .red[Uninteded] Consequences

--

.pull-left[

<iframe width="560" height="400" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/qH4qCX4GL4k?si=7jEAwuOuhRtj4TRA" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

]

--

.pull-right[

<iframe width="560" height="400" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/tKiovgr5x_c?si=7sf-hsdHLx7liPxH" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

]

---

.LARGE[ South-South grass-root network?]

--

```{r, out.width="87%", fig.align='center'}

knitr::include_graphics('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/NGOnetwork.png')

```

---

# NGO - IO working together: .hl3[synergie]? .tiny[(Jo & Simmons 2016)]

```{r, out.width="80%", fig.align='center'}

knitr::include_graphics('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/Jo2016.png')

```

---


.full-width-blue[

# What we know now.

]
<br>

--

## - IOs and NGOs are not perfect, but play important roles.

--

## - NGOs are not unicorns and prone to unintended consequences.

--

## - Still important actors for global governance.
